segments.text: But now, Job, listen to my words; pay attention to everything I say.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I am about to open my mouth; my words are on the tip of my tongue.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: My words come from an upright heart; my lips sincerely speak what I know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: The Spirit of God has made me; the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Answer me then, if you can; stand up and argue your case before me.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: I am the same as you in God's sight; I too am a piece of clay.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: No fear of me should alarm you, nor should my hand be heavy on you.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: But you have said in my hearing— I heard the very words—
I am pure, I have done no wrong; I am clean and free from sin.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet God has found fault with me; he considers me his enemy.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He fastens my feet in shackles; he keeps close watch on all my paths.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But I tell you, in this you are not right, for God is greater than any mortal.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Why do you complain to him that he responds to no one's words?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For God does speak—now one way, now another— though no one perceives it.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on people as they slumber in their beds,
he may speak in their ears and terrify them with warnings,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: to turn them from wrongdoing and keep them from pride,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: to preserve them from the pit, their lives from perishing by the sword.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Or someone may be chastened on a bed of pain with constant distress in their bones,
so that their body finds food repulsive and their soul loathes the choicest meal.
Their flesh wastes away to nothing, and their bones, once hidden, now stick out.
They draw near to the pit, and their life to the messengers of death.
Yet if there is an angel at their side, a messenger, one out of a thousand, sent to tell them how to be upright,
Spare them from going down to the pit; I have found a ransom for them—
segments.speaker: Angel/Messenger

segments.text: let their flesh be renewed like a child's; let them be restored as in the days of their youth
segments.speaker: Angel/Messenger

segments.text: then that person can pray to God and find favor with him, they will see God's face and shout for joy; he will restore them to full well-being.
I have sinned, I have perverted what is right, but I did not get what I deserved.
segments.speaker: Restored Person

segments.text: God has delivered me from going down to the pit, and I shall live to enjoy the light of life.
segments.speaker: Restored Person

segments.text: God does all these things to a person— twice, even three times—
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: to turn them back from the pit, that the light of life may shine on them.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Pay attention, Job, and listen to me; be silent, and I will speak.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If you have anything to say, answer me; speak up, for I want to vindicate you.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: But if not, then listen to me; be silent, and I will teach you wisdom.
segments.speaker: Elihu
